avoid: Tuning block size, vector width, or shared memory for the rank-16 kernel—these will not move the needle on overall performance.
Hardcoding hidden dimensions or assuming a specific d.
Attempting to write a custom full GEMM to replace cuBLAS; high risk and unlikely to beat cuBLAS for these large square matrices.
evidence: Aggregate speedup is 1.0016, essentially no improvement over baseline.
The rank-16 update (A @ (B.T @ X)) accounts for only ~0.9% of total FLOPs for d=4096, so even perfect fusion cannot yield significant overall gain.
Correctness is perfect, so performance is the only concern.
Profile summary suggests rank16_update_or_memory_bandwidth as bottleneck, but the negligible speedup indicates the full GEMM (W @ X) dominates runtime.

recommended_actions: try float4 stores when d is divisible by 4
fall back to ATen if numerical error appears
Profile to confirm that cuBLAS W @ X consumes >95% of time; if so, accept baseline as near-optimal.
If further optimization is required, explore overlapping the rank-16 update with the GEMM using a separate stream, but this adds complexity and may not help due to GPU saturation.
Consider shape-aware dispatch only if future candidates show benefit for smaller d where rank-16 fraction is larger, but for d in [3584,4608] this is not promising.
